# Project: MiniVAgent

## Overview

MiniVAgent is a node-based workflow application powered by Nano Banana Pro for building AI-driven image and text pipelines. Create flexible generation flows by connecting nodes on a visual canvas, leveraging Google Gemini models and other modern AI tools.

![MiniVAgent Screenshot](docs/screenshots/minivagent-v1.1.jpg)

![MiniVAgent Character](docs/screenshots/minivagent-v1.1-character.jpg)

## Architecture

Three-layer server stack:

```
Handler  (server/handlers/)  — HTTP only: parse request, call service, return JSON
Service  (server/services/)  — Business logic: typed options/results, calls AI APIs
Helper   (server/helpers/)   — Graph traversal, image saving, shared utilities
```

- **Handlers** never contain business logic.
- **Services** never import from `express`.
- **Helpers** are stateless utilities shared across services and handlers.

### Tech Stack

- **Backend:** Node.js, Express, TypeScript
- **Frontend:** React, TypeScript, Vite
- **AI Provider:** Google Gemini API (text, image, vision, planning); OpenAI API (optional — text, image)
- **Storage:** Local filesystem (graphs as JSON, resources as images)

## Project Structure

See [docs/project-structure.md](docs/project-structure.md) for a full breakdown of the directory layout.

## Node Types

| Type           | Purpose                        | Inputs              | Outputs            |
|----------------|--------------------------------|---------------------|--------------------|
| `TEXT_GEN`     | AI text generation             | `prompt`            | `prompt`, `output`  |
| `IMAGE_GEN`    | AI image generation            | `prompt`, `image`   | `image-0`…`image-3` |
| `IMAGE_SOURCE` | Upload or URL image input      | —                   | `image`             |
| `IMAGE_TO_TEXT`| Vision / image analysis        | `prompt`, `image`   | `output`            |
| `NOTE`         | Static text / documentation    | —                   | `prompt`            |
| `COMPARE`      | Side-by-side image comparison  | `image` (×2)        | `image-0`, `image-1`|
| `SPLIT_TEXT`   | Split text by separator        | `prompt`            | `split-0`…`split-N` |

## Graph Execution

Nodes execute in dependency order. Connected text and images are resolved by traversing the edge graph before each node runs.

**Prompt merging rules:**
- `TEXT_GEN`: `node.prompt + "\n\n--- Context ---\n" + connectedText`
- `IMAGE_GEN`: `node.prompt + " " + connectedText`

**Reference image resolution:**

| Format | Resolution |
|--------|-----------|
| `resource:<uuid>` | Read from `data/resources/` |
| `http://` / `https://` | Fetched at runtime |
| `data:…;base64,…` | Passed through unchanged |

**Node result shape** (same across `/run` and `/run/stream`):

```json
{
  "id": "node-id",
  "type": "TEXT_GEN",
  "data": { "prompt": "..." },
  "outputAssets": [
    { "type": "text", "value": "..." }
  ],
  "error": null
}
```

- `data` holds input/config: `{ prompt }` for TEXT_GEN, IMAGE_GEN, IMAGE_TO_TEXT, NOTE; `{}` otherwise
- `outputAssets` holds generated results:
  - Text: `{ type, value }`
  - Image: `{ type, mimeType, size, sizeStr, resourceId, url }`

**SSE events** (`/run/stream`):

| Event | Payload |
|-------|---------|
| `job_start` | `{ jobId, graphId, graphName, nodeCount, edgeCount, executionOrder }` |
| `node_start` | `{ nodeId, type }` |
| `node_end` | Full node result object |
| `job_done` | `{ status, graphId, graphName, nodeCount, edgeCount, executedNodes, elapsed, nodes, edges }` |
| `job_error` | `{ error }` |

## API Endpoints

See [docs/api-endpoints.md](docs/api-endpoints.md) for the full list of available endpoints.

## Setup & Commands

### Prerequisites

- Node.js v18+
- Google Gemini API key (required)
- OpenAI API key (optional — for GPT text and image models)

### Install & Run

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Create .env.local with your API key
echo "GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here" > .env.local

# Development mode (hot reload, port 3202)
npm run dev

# Production build + serve (port 3201)
npm run dev:compiled
```

### Tests

```bash
npm test
npm run test:watch
npm run test:coverage
```

## AI-Friendly

This project provides context for AI and LLM agents via [`llms.txt`](llms.txt), enabling its architecture and usage to be easily understood and indexed.
